fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: South Carolina Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to South Carolina - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity south carolina, how to transfer pharmacist license to south carolina, pharmacist compact south carolina, south carolina pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: South Carolina Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: South Carolina Department of Labor, Licensing and Regulation (LLR)
board.mailing_address: South Carolina Board of Pharmacy, P.O. Box 11929, Columbia, SC 29211-1929

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in South Carolina.

steps.title: Access the LLR Online Services Portal
steps.description: Navigate to the LLR Online Services Portal, select 'Board of Pharmacy' and 'Pharmacist' as your application type. Create an account or log in to begin the application process.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Fill out the comprehensive online pharmacist application through the LLR Online Services Portal, providing personal, educational, and professional history details.

steps.title: Pay the Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable pharmacist application fee of $125 via the LLR Online Services Portal as part of the application submission.

steps.title: Arrange for NABP E-licensure Verification
steps.description: If applying by endorsement, request NABP to send your official E-licensure verification directly to the South Carolina Board of Pharmacy from all states where you are or have been licensed.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Ensure your official pharmacy school transcripts are sent directly from your college or university to the South Carolina Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided by the Board after your application submission to complete the mandatory criminal background check, which typically involves fingerprinting.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a South Carolina pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does South Carolina have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in South Carolina. To work in South Carolina, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to South Carolina?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in South Carolina while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in South Carolina
hero.dek: To apply for a pharmacist license in South Carolina, applicants must use the LLR Online Services Portal to complete and submit their application, including all required documentation for licensure by endorsement (reciprocity) or examination.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the LLR Online Services Portal, select 'Board of Pharmacy' from the board dropdown, and then 'Pharmacist' as the application type. Click 'Continue' and you will be prompted to log in or create a new user account if you do not have one.
